intro: Ebnerella boedekeriana, also known as the Boedeker's wormwood or Boedeker's wormwood plant, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from digestive issues, such as indigestion and nausea, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The bioactive constituents of Ebnerella boedekeriana include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to alleviate digestive discomfort and promote overall well-being.
